_meta.title: Milestone 0 — Portal Ready
_meta.purpose: First deployable version of the green-ai platform. Everything needed for a developer to log in, navigate to a workspace, and make an authenticated API call. No domain features (no SMS, no broadcast, no recipients). Pure platform.
_meta.deployment_target: Azure App Service + Azure SQL + Azure Blob Storage

definition_of_done: A developer can: (1) be invited by email, (2) set their password, (3) log in with email+password, (4) select their workspace if multiple, (5) make an authenticated GET API call, (6) log out. ALL of this works without errors, warnings, or security issues.
seed_data_required: LocaleStringResources: minimum 200 labels (login, nav, errors, form validation)
1 SuperAdmin User linked to operator Tenant
non_functional_requirements: All credentials via IApplicationSettingService — never hardcoded or from appsettings.json in service code
ICurrentUser: exactly 1 implementation in HTTP context. Architectural test enforces this.
All feature services use ISystemLogger — never ILogger<T> or Serilog directly
TenantId=0 in JWT → HTTP 401 from CurrentUserMiddleware — no bypass
WorkspaceId=0 in JWT → HTTP 401 — no bypass
GET /health returns 200 with no auth — load balancer ready
security_review_checklist: No plaintext passwords in DB — only Argon2id hash + salt
No plaintext API keys anywhere — hash only
Password reset token: 15-min expiry, GUID, invalidated after use
UserRefreshTokens cleaned up on logout and on token reuse attack detection
Request body logging redacts password and token fields

acceptance_test_scenarios.name: Happy path login
steps: SuperAdmin invites new user via POST /api/users
User receives invite email with token
User logs in with email+password → receives accessToken + refreshToken
User calls GET /api/localization/languages with accessToken → 200 OK
User calls GET /api/auth/refresh → receives new accessToken
acceptance_test_scenarios.expected: All steps return correct HTTP status codes. 0 errors in Logs table.

acceptance_test_scenarios.name: Lockout protection
acceptance_test_scenarios.expected: 7th attempt returns HTTP 403 (locked out). IsLockedOut=true in DB.

acceptance_test_scenarios.name: Workspace selection required
steps: User receives new token with WorkspaceId set
acceptance_test_scenarios.expected: HTTP 300 on login triggers workspace selection. After selection, 200 on all authenticated endpoints.

acceptance_test_scenarios.name: Health endpoint
acceptance_test_scenarios.expected: 200 when DB online. 503 when DB offline. No credentials exposed in either response.

acceptance_test_scenarios.name: Unauthorized tenant access blocked
steps: Manually forge JWT with TenantB id
acceptance_test_scenarios.expected: CurrentUserMiddleware verifies DB User.CurrentTenantId. JWT claim cannot be forged if user.CurrentTenantId is authoritative. Return 401.
